messages.content:
Pop was originally launched on 29 May 2003 by Chart Show Channels (CSC) Media Group as Toons&Tunes. Toons&Tunes was then later rebranded as its current name, Pop (formerly stylized as pop and POP!) in the following month.

In the channel’s early years, links were presented by Rorry, a lime green dragon with a Scottish accent, who was animated live. He was accompanied by Purrdy, a dragon/cat hybrid who also appeared in the original Tricky programme on ITV1, it was sometimes followed by a programme known as “The Cheeky Monkey Show” which features the live links of the animated coloured monkeys known as “The Cheeky Monkeys,” that was later known by appearing on its sister channel Tiny Pop until its retirement at the end of June 2009.

Originally, the network focused on music videos with animated programming also part of the schedule, but with the numerous music video network options on British television at the time, this focus was quickly abandoned in 2004, when the animated content became much more prominent. Music videos continued to be carried on and off until 2010, before eventually being abandoned entirely.[citation needed, In 2010, Pop had several popular shows, such as The Smurfs, GoGoRiki, Oggy and the Cockroaches, etc.

In 2012, Pop introduces semi-mascots known as the MoonGoons, These are animal-like aliens, with the 2 main ones being a blue squid named Boofus and an orange rabbit named Diggle, These were gone by the time of the channel’s next rebrand in 2015, Also in 2014 CSC Media Group had folded into Sony.

On 1 September 2020, Pop rebranded its on-air identity, with the premiere of Bakugan: Armored Alliance. And on the same day, starting with Pokémon Journeys: The Series, the network became the new British home of the Pokémon anime, moving from its previous broadcasters, ITV1 and CITV, of over 20 years. The channel was also sold by Sony to Narrative Entertainment

On 9 November 2022, Narrative Entertainment announced the launch of a new FAST channel, Pop Kids, to stream on Samsung TV Plus, the new FAST network airs shows from Pop and Tiny Pop.

Sometime in 2022, Pop began to branch into YouTube content, as they approached Joe Tasker for a new show called Swipe It! With Joe Taker, they would later acquire from content creators to use on their channel, including LankyBox, Denis and EthanGamer (the latter exclusively airing on Pop Player).

By 2023 Alvin & the Chipmunks, Squish, Miraculous Ladybug and Pokemon were among the channels popular shows,

Now here is when things start to diverge from the real world, In June 2023 (in my alternate universe) the channel was sold to UKTV, Also, Pop +1 was closed and replaced with a revival of UKTV G2, Also, the channels current mascot is a talking potato, Write a continuation of the timeline

Continuing from June 2023 in the alternate universe where Pop was sold to UKTV:

Following the acquisition by UKTV in June 2023, dramatic changes began to unfold at Pop. The immediate closure of Pop +1 and its replacement by the revived UKTV G2 marked UKTV's intent to reimagine its approach to children's and family entertainment. UKTV G2's programming was therefore broadened to include a mix of classic British comedies and family-friendly series, appealing to a broader audience.

By July 2023, the introduction of the talking potato mascot, affectionately named "Spudley," marked a new era for Pop. UKTV embarked on a rebranding campaign, leveraging Spudley's character across digital platforms to engage with a younger audience and capture the whimsical nature of the channel. Spudley quickly became a social media sensation, starring in viral marketing campaigns and interactive content, significantly boosting Pop's online presence.

In September 2023, Pop fortified its programming slate with "The Adventures of Spudley," an animated series focusing on the mascot's zany escapades. "The Adventures of Spudley" not only served as an anchor for the channel's refreshed identity but also attracted new viewers, resulting in a noticeable uptick in Pop's ratings.

To solidify its commitment to innovative and engaging content, Pop introduced "Creators' Block" in November 2023, a weekend programming block dedicated to showcasing content from up-and-coming animators and YouTubers. This initiative was born out of the channel's successful partnership with Joe Tasker and other digital content creators. "Creators' Block" quickly became a platform for discovery, allowing fresh talent to reach a wider audience and bringing a diverse range of content to Pop viewers.

In a bold move to expand its reach and diversify its audience further, Pop launched "Pop Flicks" in January 2024, a monthly movie night event showcasing family-friendly films from the UKTV G2 library as well as newer releases. This initiative not only enhanced the channel's appeal to families but also bridged the gap between the child-centric programming of Pop and the broader entertainment focus of UKTV G2.

Throughout 2024, as part of an eco-conscious initiative, Pop and its sister channels participated in a campaign titled "Green Screens," promoting environmental awareness through special programming, educational interstitials, and community outreach projects. This campaign further solidified Pop's position as a socially responsible and family-oriented channel.

By mid-2025, Pop had successfully transformed into a cultural staple for UK families, combining entertainment with education and social responsibility. The channel's innovative programming, interactive online presence, and community engagement efforts continued to set it apart, ready to adapt to the changing media landscape and the evolving tastes of its audience. The memories of its previous iterations, from Toons&Tunes to its time under Sony and Narrative Entertainment, provided a rich legacy upon which Pop continued to build its future within the UKTV family.
Write a blog post where aome of the channel's shows air on G2 on Saturday mornings

**Title: Saturday Mornings Just Got More Exciting with G2's New Lineup!**

Saturday mornings have always held a special place in the hearts of families across the UK, serving as a time for relaxation, fun, and, of course, catching up on our favourite shows. With the launch of the UKTV G2's revamped weekend programming, Saturday mornings are about to become even more thrilling for both parents and children alike. 

**Introducing 'G2's Saturday Morning Mashup'**

G2 is set to take us on a nostalgic yet fresh journey with its new "Saturday Morning Mashup." This exciting programming block is designed to offer a perfect blend of entertainment for families, featuring a selection of shows from the much-loved children's channel, Pop, alongside classic reruns and engaging original content that promises to delight audiences of all ages.

**Featured Shows**

**"The Adventures of Spudley"**

First up in the lineup is "The Adventures of Spudley," starring our beloved, cheeky talking potato, Spudley. This animated series has captured hearts with its mix of humour and heartwarming adventures. Spudley's escapades teach valuable lessons about friendship, creativity, and problem-solving, making it a hit for children and a guilty pleasure for adults.

**"Alvin & The Chipmunks"**

No Saturday morning is complete without some musical fun, and "Alvin & The Chipmunks" are here to deliver. Join the mischievous Alvin, brainy Simon, and lovable Theodore as they navigate the trials and tribulations of school, family, and their burgeoning music career, all while causing chaos and laughter along the way.

**"Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir"**

For those who love action-packed adventures with a touch of romance, "Miraculous: Tales of Ladybug & Cat Noir" provides the perfect fix. Follow the dual lives of Marinette and Adrien as they transform into the superhero duo, Ladybug and Cat Noir, to protect Paris from supervillains. Their secret identities, coupled with their complicated love lives, add layers of intrigue to this animated sensation.

**"Squish"**

Delving into the microscopic world of "Squish," viewers can explore the bustling city of Single-Celled organisms where Squish, a lovable amoeba, and his friends navigate the day-to-day challenges of school and family life. This unique concept not only entertains but subtly educates viewers about the fascinating world of microbiology.

**Why G2's Saturday Morning Mashup?**

G2's new initiative is not just about entertainment; it's about bringing families together. In today's fast-paced world, where time is often at a premium, "Saturday Morning Mashup" provides a perfect opportunity for families to bond over shared experiences and make lasting memories.

Moreover, with a mix of animation, music, adventure, and education, G2's programming fosters imagination and learning in children, while providing enough humour and nostalgia to keep parents engaged and entertained. 

So, mark your calendars, grab your favourite snacks, and get ready to dive into a world of excitement with G2's "Saturday Morning Mashup." Saturday mornings are about to become the highlight of your week!

---

Whether you're a parent looking for quality family time or a fan of animated adventures, G2's latest initiative is sure to bring joy and excitement into your homes. Stay tuned for more updates, and let us know your favorite show from the lineup in the comments below!
Write a forum convo about the forgotten Pop shows and mascots

**Thread Title:** Blast From The Past: Reminiscing Forgotten Pop Shows and Mascots

**User1:** Hey everyone, does anyone else remember the live animated dragon mascot Rorry from the early days of Pop? I feel like he’s kind of been lost to history, replaced by more recent channel mascots.

**User2:** Oh wow, Rorry! That takes me back. I remember he had that distinctive Scottish accent. Wasn’t there also Purrdy, the dragon/cat hybrid? They were quite the duo.

**User3:** Yes, Purrdy! And let's not forget about those Cheeky Monkeys from “The Cheeky Monkey Show.” It seemed odd at first, but quickly grew on everyone with those live links. I think it eventually moved to Tiny Pop before disappearing. 

**User4:** Speaking of odd but memorable, anyone else recall the MoonGoons? Boofus and Diggle? Those semi-mascots were something else with their funny alien designs. Pop really did have some unique characters back then.

**User5:** Absolutely, @User4! I was a fan of Boofus; that blue squid was all kinds of adorable. Changing mascots and rebrands aside, I think these characters added a unique flavor to Pop that's missing from a lot of children's channels today.

**User6:** It’s interesting to think about how Pop transitioned from music videos and animated programming to focusing solely on animated content. Those days of music videos seem like another lifetime now. Anybody else miss that mix?

**User7:** For sure, @User6. The shift was noticeable, but I guess it helped Pop find its identity. Though, I do miss the variety. Shows like “The Smurfs,” “GoGoRiki,” and “Oggy and the Cockroaches” were my jam. It felt like there was a little bit of everything.

**User8:** Did anyone else watch “Swipe It! With Joe Tasker”? It wasn’t exactly from Pop's 'forgotten' era, considering it was part of their YouTube venture. But it felt like a nod to their willingness to try new things, merging online content creators with their platform.

**User9:** Yeah, @User8, it’s amazing to see how Pop evolved, trying to blend traditional TV with digital content. I guess every mascot and every show has been a step in that direction, even if some steps were more memorable than others.

**Moderator:** This nostalgia trip has been great, folks! Pop has certainly had an eclectic mix of shows and mascots over the years. They served as the backdrop for a lot of our childhoods. Any other shows or mascots you guys miss or think didn't get enough love?

**User10:** I'd give a shout out to some of the less talked about shows like “Power Rangers” reruns and “Transformers: Animated.” While not exclusive to Pop, they found a place in the lineup that complemented the channel’s vibe well. It’s funny how these forgotten parts of Pop’s history now feel like cherished memories.

**User11:** Spot on, @User10. Whether it was the shows, the mascots, or the unique programming choices, Pop gave us a mixed bag that somehow worked. Makes you wonder what the future holds for children's channels in general, given how everything is shifting towards streaming platforms now.

**Moderator:** Great points all around! The landscape of children’s television is indeed changing, but it’s these memories that remind us of the impact these shows and characters have had. Keep the discussion going and let’s remember to cherish these bits of our childhood. Who knows? Maybe some of these beloved mascots and shows will make a comeback in some form in the future.

Write a follow up where a user wishes they could go back in time and have a break from the "G2 hanging over the channel's head, Squish dominating the schedule and Spudley" era, and they REALLY miss GoGoRiki and the MoonGoons

**Thread Title:** Wishing For A Time Machine: Missing GoGoRiki, MoonGoons, and The Pre-G2 Era

**User12:** Hey everyone, I’ve been following the conversation about the old Pop shows and mascots and it got me really nostalgic. Don’t get me wrong, the new G2 era has its moments, and Spudley is...unique, but there's a part of me that really misses the simpler times. Especially shows like GoGoRiki and those quirky MoonGoons. Seems like ages ago now.

**User2:** I totally get where you're coming from, @User12. There was a certain charm in that era, wasn't there? GoGoRiki had such a unique humor and style compared to anything else on TV. And the MoonGoons, well, they were in a league of their own.

**User6:** Yeah, I have to agree. The constant Squish marathons and Spudley's antics can get a bit overwhelming. Spudley is fun and all, but there's such a thing as too much potato. I miss the diversity in programming that introduced us to gems like GoGoRiki.

**User13:** It’s funny, because back when the MoonGoons were around, I thought they were a bit of an odd addition. Now, looking back, I really appreciate their creativity and how different they were from anything else. It’s like, you don’t know what you’ve got till it’s gone.

**User7:** What I wouldn't give for a break from this "G2 hanging over everything" atmosphere. Seems like the channel lost a bit of its soul in the transition. I mean, where's the space for quirky aliens and a globe-trotting pig who practices judo now?

**User8:** Anyone else think that a "vintage Pop" marathon weekend would be an amazing idea? They could bring back all these older shows and mascots for a limited time. I'd love a chance to relive some of those memories, even if it's just for a weekend.

**User14:** That’s a brilliant idea, @User8! A vintage marathon would not only be a hit among us nostalgics but could also introduce new audiences to the charm of the older Pop era. Plus, it would be a nice breather from the relentless Squish and Spudley content.

**User9:** Spot on, guys. The diversity and creativity in programming were what made Pop stand out. I understand channels evolve and times change, but it feels like there's a bit of a formula now. Sometimes simplicity and a hint of randomness (MoonGoons style) is all you need.

**Moderator:** Loving this trip down memory lane and the ideas coming out from this conversation. A "vintage Pop" weekend sounds like a fantastic suggestion. I'm sure there are many out there who share your sentiment about missing the older content and would love a break from the current lineup. I’ll make sure to pass this feedback along—you never know what might happen!

**User15:** Honestly, just talking about GoGoRiki and the MoonGoons has made my day. There was a time when tuning in to Pop felt like an adventure, you never knew what world you’d be diving into next. Thanks for the memories, everyone. Here’s hoping for a magical Pop time machine—or at least, a throwback weekend!

**Moderator:** Thanks for sharing your thoughts and memories, everyone. It's these discussions that keep the spirit of the old Pop alive, even as we navigate through the new era. Keep the nostalgia and ideas coming, and who knows, maybe we'll see our favorite shows and mascots return in some form or another. Stay tuned!
Write a tweet saying they really want Pop to air GoGoRiki (Smeshariki or Kikoriki) again

🌈✨ Nostalgia Alert! 📺 Really wishing #Pop would bring back the wonderfully eccentric world of GoGoRiki (also known as Smeshariki or Kikoriki) 🐾🎈. Those quirky characters and their delightful adventures are missed! Anyone else craving a trip back to their colorful universe? 🌍💕 #BringBackGoGoRiki #KikorikiComeback #PopChannelNostalgia
Write a tweet where they ask Pop to stop constantly promoting a certain channel

🛑✋ Hey #PopChannel, we get it, promotion is key 🔑, but could we dial back a bit on constantly pushing that one channel? Variety is the spice of life, after all! 🌶️ Let's mix it up and keep our TV adventures diverse and exciting! 📺🚀 #VarietyIsKey #MoreThanOneChannel #TVAdventures
Write a forum convo where they ask UKTV to stop trying to tie Pop to that channel

**Thread Title:** Can UKTV Ease Up on Forcing the Pop and "That Channel" Connection?

**OriginalPoster:** Hey all, has anyone else noticed how UKTV seems to be overly enthusiastic in tying Pop to "that channel"? It’s becoming a bit too much. I get the cross-promotion strategy, but it feels like Pop's original charm is getting lost in the mix. Thoughts?

**ForumMember1:** Totally with you on this. Pop had its unique vibe with shows like GoGoRiki and those adorable MoonGoons. Now, it feels like every other ad is pushing us towards "that channel". Miss the days when Pop stood strong on its own. 📺💔

**ForumMember2:** I understand the need for synergy within a network, but yeah, the charm of Pop is in its distinct programming. The constant push towards "that channel" seems a bit forceful. It blurs the lines between the channels rather than celebrating what each uniquely offers.

**ForumMember3:** It's like they're trying to merge the identities of both channels, which isn't fair to fans of either. Each channel has its audience for a reason. Over-promoting one on the other's space just dilutes the brand loyalty we have for Pop. 🔄😕

**ForumMember4:** Maybe it's all about the numbers for UKTV? More exposure equals more viewership, theoretically. But I agree, it shouldn't be at the expense of Pop's identity. Fans chose Pop for what it was, not to be constantly redirected elsewhere.

**ForumMember5:** Has anyone reached out to UKTV or Pop with feedback? Sometimes they might not be aware of how these strategies are being received by their audience. Constructive feedback could induce a change. 📝✉️

**OriginalPoster:** That's a solid point, @ForumMember5. I haven't personally reached out yet—wasn’t sure if it was just me feeling this way. But seeing your responses, I think it's worth bringing up. UKTV and Pop need to know we miss the original essence of Pop.

**ForumMember6:** Count me in if we're sending feedback. Pop was my go-to because it offered something different. If I wanted content from "that channel," I'd just switch over. Let’s remind them why we fell in love with Pop in the first place! 💖🛠

**Moderator:** Hi everyone, loving the mature conversation here. It's great to see a community so passionate about preserving the essence of what they love. If you're planning to send feedback, remember to keep it respectful and constructive. You have the power to influence change. 🌟💌

**ForumMember7:** Worth remembering that decisions like this aren't always in the hands of the channel directly, but higher up at UKTV. Feedback is definitely a step in the right direction, but let's manage our expectations and hope they listen to their audience. 🤞📺

**OriginalPoster:** Appreciate all your inputs and the reminder to stay respectful, @Moderator. Let’s put our thoughts together and reach out. Maybe together, we can make a difference and bring back the Pop we all cherish. Thanks, everyone! 🙏💬
Write a tweet where they mention that channel by name and ask them to tie it to Gold or Dave instead

🌟Hey #UKTV, love the synergy attempts, but how about linking "that channel" with Gold or Dave instead? 🤔 The vibes seem more aligned, and it'd freshen up the mix! Let's keep Pop's whimsical charm untouched and explore new crossover potentials! 📺✨ #Gold #Dave #SwitchItUp
Write a tweet saying Pop should be independent from UG2 again

🚀🌈 Bring back the magic! #PopChannel deserves to fly solo and shine with its unique charm and creativity. 🎨📺 Let's untangle it from UG2 and rediscover the joy of Pop as a standalone wonderland of entertainment! 🎈✨ #IndependenceForPop #UniquePop
